I treated the 41 prior turns as a proposal log, not as proof. The prompt fixes the submission format with distinguished sigma,tau, implicit tame relation tau^sigma=tau^2, later generators in the pro-2 core, and exponents reduced modulo M=85667662080.The only still-live candidate in the prior log is the dynamic tame-inertia word from Turns 9‚Äì41; the earlier scalar, plain-œÄ2, and fixed-œÑ‚àí2 candidates were refuted there by explicit finite-tame or index-two tests.

The live candidate remains variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with œÄ=œÄ2‚ààZ and Œ∑=2œÄ on odd tame inertia,r=xœÉ‚àíœÄxœÉœÄx‚àí1(xœÑ‚àíŒ∑)œÄ ((yœÑ)œÄ)œÉy‚àí1,where œÄ‚â°40491355905,‚àíœÄ‚â°45176306175,‚àíŒ∑‚â°50932630783(modM).The outside structural benchmark is unchanged: Neukirch‚Äôs report says the Jannsen‚ÄìWingberg proof for the known pÓÄÝ=2 case works by passing to finite normal tame subextensions, checking the maximal p-extension kernels as Demu≈°kin groups, tracking the finite tame action on units, and then applying a Koch/Demu≈°kin-formation uniqueness theorem.Numdam Jarden‚ÄìShusterman also record that Jannsen‚ÄìWingberg give an explicit description for pÓÄÝ=2, that Diekert‚Äôs dyadic analogue does not cover K=Q2, and that Gal(K) has minimal generator number [K:Qp]+2 and relation number [K:Qp]+1.Mathematics at TAU The important new audit point is that the last several turns still had the key sign-cover fraction reversed. This matters because division by 1‚àít is not legitimate in the 2-adic sign-cover group algebra.Let V=kerŒ∫ be the sign double cover of a finite-tame pro-2 kernel, let t be its deck involution, and work on a nontrivial odd tame-inertia character block where œÑ‚Ü¶Œ±ÓÄÝ=1. For the norm wordNa (u)=(uœÑa)œÄ,the Fox coefficient is the completed geometric seriesDa=j=0‚àëœÄ‚àí1(tŒ±a)j.

In the completed group algebra the identity is (1‚àítŒ±a)Da=1‚àí(tŒ±a)œÄ.Since œÄ‚â°1(mod2) and œÄ‚â°0(mode) on an e-th tame-character block,(tŒ±a)œÄ=t.

Therefore the correct formula isDa=1‚àítŒ±a1‚àít.This is the opposite of the formula displayed at the end of Turn 41. The corrected direction is exactly what makes the unit/radical argument work: 1‚àítŒ±a is a unit because Œ±aÓÄÝ=1, while 1‚àít lies in the radical. Hence Da is radical, and the coefficient contributed by the correction factorx‚àí1(xœÑ‚àíŒ∑)œÄis‚àí1+D‚àíŒ∑,a unit. Thus the intended conclusion survives: nontrivial odd tame-character blocks contribute only unit Smith pivots to the sign-kernel abelianization. But the usable formula is (1‚àít)/(1‚àítŒ±a), not (1‚àítŒ±a)/(1‚àít).There is a parallel correction in the trivial tame-inertia block. With T the œÉ-cycle operator and s‚â°œÄ(modf), the mod-2 linear relation is(1+T‚àís)X+(1+T)Y=0.

Write f=2a m, m odd. Then s‚â°1(mod2a) and s‚â°0(modm). If f is even, s is odd, so 1+T‚à£1+T‚àís. The legitimate elimination is thereforeY=Ps (T)X+YÀâ,Ps(T)=1+T1+T‚àís,up to the harmless convention T‚ÜîT‚àí1. The reciprocal is not valid in A=F2 [T]/(Tf‚àí1).With h(T)=1+Ps (T)+Ps(T‚àí1),for a nontrivial f-th root Œ∂ one gets (1+Œ∂)h(Œ∂)=Œ∂‚àís+Œ∂s+1=Œ∂‚àís(1+Œ∂2s+1).Since 2s+1 is odd and congruent to 1(modm), gcd(f,2s+1)=1. Thus h has no nontrivial f-th-root zero, and h(1)=1. So the trivial block nonsingularity argument also survives, but only with Ps=(1+T‚àís)/(1+T).With these corrected formulas, the prior finite-tame abelianization statements remain plausible and internally consistent. For a legal tame quotientŒîe,f=‚ü®œÉÀâ,œÑÀâ‚à£œÑÀâe=1,œÉÀâf=1,œÉÀâœÑÀâœÉÀâ‚àí1=œÑÀâ2‚ü©,the candidate still givesUe,fab‚âÖZ2ef+1‚äïC2,and for the sign kernel,(kerŒ∫)ab‚âÖZ22ef+1‚äïC4.

The C2 and C4 torsion lies in the tame-inertia-invariant block; the nontrivial odd tame-character blocks are unit-pivot blocks. What is actually proved here is the corrected algebra behind that conclusion, not the full profinite presentation.I also slightly reframe the mixed (e,f)=(3,2) target. The prior candidate-side computation should be compared only after fixing a tame Frobenius lift. In the S3 -quotient, withAi=XœÑi,Bi=XœÑiœÉ,i‚ààZ/3,andZd=i‚ààZ/3‚àë[Ai,Bi+d],the candidate‚Äôs first mixed even-Frobenius class is represented byŒ¥d (b)=Zd,Œ¥d(Ai)=[Bi+d,S],Œ¥d(Bi+d)=[Ai,S],Œ¥d (S)=0.The label d=0 is not intrinsic: replacing the Frobenius lift by œÉ‚Ä≤=œÑk œÉ cycles Zd‚Ü¶Zd+k. Thus the invariant arithmetic target is the orbit {Œ¥0,Œ¥1,Œ¥2}, and the sharper target Œ¥0 only makes sense after choosing the same standard tame Frobenius lift on the arithmetic side.So I would not mark the candidate as solved. The present state is: the live word has survived many finite-tame kernel, cup-product, abelianization, sign-kernel, and low filtered-action tests, but the actual missing theorem is still the dyadic formation/rigidity step: one must identify the candidate‚Äôs exact filtered (œÉ,œÑ)-operator action with the arithmetic Frobenius/tame-inertia action over every finite tame subextension.

ESTABLISHED: The live dynamic candidate remains unrefuted. I corrected the two recurring reciprocal errors. The correct formulas areDa=1‚àítŒ±a1‚àít,Ps(T)=1+T1+T‚àís (up to T‚ÜîT‚àí1). With these directions, the nontrivial tame-character sign-cover blocks are unit-pivot blocks, and the prior conclusions Ue,fab‚âÖZ2ef+1‚äïC2 and (kerŒ∫)ab‚âÖZ22ef+1‚äïC4 survive.

GAP: This is still not a proof of the presentation of GQ2. The missing ingredient is a dyadic formation/rigidity theorem, or an explicit arithmetic comparison, proving that the candidate‚Äôs exact filtered tame action realizes the actual arithmetic Frobenius/tame-inertia action for all finite tame subextensions.

NEXT: Fix a concrete S3 -extension, such as L=Q2(Œ∂3,32), and a Frobenius-lift convention. Compute the arithmetic action on GL (2)/GL(2)3 or GL (2)/GL(2)5, with the precise target that it matches the candidate‚Äôs relation-preserving class Œ¥0 for that lift, or one of Œ¥0,Œ¥1,Œ¥2 before fixing the lift.[BREAKTHROUGH]
